title: Pilosella castellana
taxonomy: Pilosella castellana is classified within the family Rosaceae and the genus Pilosella. It belongs to the tribe Senecioneae. Further classification places it in the subfamily Cichorioideae. Additionally, it is a part of the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. Pilosella castellana is a member of the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Pilosella castellana, also known as the Castilian hawk's beard, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. Its leaves and stems are reported to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its potential use in treating various conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption. The plant's root is said to have astringent properties, which could be beneficial for treating skin irritations or wounds. Further studies are required to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Pilosella castellana.
